title: Composters Boost Crops! (CropBooster)
description: Upgrade Composters to boost surrounding crops, since 1.17, with detailed in-game settings!
body:
Existed since 1.17, CropBooster adds an optional Composter upgrade that lets you boost nearby crops!
# 📜 Features: - **Composters boost nearby crops**! - You first need to upgrade it with a Bone Block first. - This is so that the regular Composter is still there for those who needs it. - **Supports all Minecraft crops**! - This includes all crops that grow naturally, and those that grows on Bone Meal! - **In-game Settings**: Customize CropBooster to fit your worlds! - Tweak the stats on every supported crop, and more! - To get started: `/function tdp.config:cropbooster` - Works well with [my other datapacks](https://modrinth.com/collection/Zz40EjyL)! - Works in Multiplayer! - Made and maintained with lots of care. Hope you enjoyed it!
![An animated icon showcasing CropBooster's core premise.](https://github.com/TheDiamondPlayables/assets2/blob/main/Datapacks/cropbooster/icon_animated.gif?raw=true)
# 🤔 ***It's not working, help!*** - If you can't access in-game settings or debug commands, **open to LAN and enable Allow Commands**! - If you're in a Server, you'll need to `/op` yourself first! - Before 1.20.5, this option was called **Allow Cheats**. - For any other problems, you can [contact me personally here](https://thediamondplayables.com/contact)! - I'll personally respond to all help requests that's been sent here. Reply times may vary - there's just me working on all this, but I'll always help you out when I can. # 🎉 Special Thanks: Special thanks to these awesome people for keeping the lights on: [![A special thank you board for those who helped me keep the lights on, so I get to do this for you!](https://thediamondplayables.com/helpers)](https://thediamondplayables.com/support) If you like the effort that goes into making all this, [help me keep the lights on](https://thediamondplayables.com/support) so I get to do this more often! # 📝 PS: - Each version **was designed and optimized** for their respective Minecraft release, so you'll always **get the best performance and features possible** for the releases you're playing on. - I could make builds that supports multiple versions at once, but it also means adding extra stuff only to support game releases that it won't be run on. It just doesn't make sense to me. - Due to [Modrinth's limitations](https://thediamondplayables.com/modrinth_notes), this version is **not the best of what I've got**. [![Modrinth Edition notice. It's a simpler version made due to Modrinth's tech limits. You don't get all the features if you're using this release, and some things may not work as intended.](https://github.com/TheDiamondPlayables/assets2/blob/main/Random/modrinth_notice.gif?raw=true)](https://thediamondplayables.com/modrinth_notes)
